title: compare-guidelines-output
description: Comparison of a biomedical paper (or set of papers) against current clinical practice guidelines

clinical_topic.description: Clinical topic of the paper used to select relevant guidelines

paper_summary_for_comparison.description: One-paragraph summary of the paper's question and main finding, written for the guideline-comparison context

relevant_guidelines.description: Each guideline relevant to the paper's topic. Empty array if no major guideline covers the topic.

society.description: Issuing society or organization (e.g., SEPAR, ATS, ERS, GOLD, GINA, IASLC, NICE, AASM)

year.description: Publication or last-update year of the cited guideline version

specific_recommendation_quoted.description: The recommendation text affected by the paper. Preserve original language.

recommendation_is_paraphrased.description: True if the recommendation is paraphrased rather than quoted verbatim

rationale.description: Why this alignment was chosen for this guideline

guideline_url.description: URL of the guideline source (society website or DOI)

overall_alignment.description: Overall classification across all cited guidelines. Use 'mixed' when guidelines diverge.

relative_evidence_strength.description: Strength of evidence in the paper vs strength of evidence behind current recommendation(s)

knowledge_cutoff_caveat.description: Mandatory statement that guidelines may have been updated since the model's training cutoff and that the user should verify the live source

confidence.description: Confidence in the comparison (independent of strength of evidence in either the paper or the guideline)

comparison_notes.description: Free-text notes: divergences between societies, topics not covered, approximate quotations, model uncertainty
